headline: How The Investment Story For Keurig Dr Pepper (KDP) Is Shifting With Mixed Street Signals
summary: Keurig Dr Pepper’s modeled fair value has been trimmed from US$35.29 to US$33.25, a roughly 5.8% adjustment that reflects a more measured stance on what the shares might be worth. This move lines up with recent Street research, where some banks have lifted price targets into the high US$30s while others have cut targets by US$3 to US$6 and leaned more cautious. Read on to see how these shifting targets fit together and how you can track the evolving story around the stock. Analyst Price...

headline: Wells Fargo (WFC) Valuation Check As Card Spending Softens And Net Interest Income Concerns Grow
summary: Wells Fargo (WFC) is back in focus after its latest card spending data showed daily credit and debit usage turning nearly flat year over year, excluding gasoline, which hints at softer consumer activity ahead. See our latest analysis for Wells Fargo. At a share price of US$81.50, Wells Fargo has seen a 5.58% 1 month share price return but a 14.39% decline year to date. The 1 year total shareholder return of 17.12% and 3 year total shareholder return of over 7x reflect much stronger longer...

headline: 3 Reasons to Avoid WFC and 1 Stock to Buy Instead
summary: Over the past six months, Wells Fargo’s shares (currently trading at $80.54) have posted a disappointing 7.3% loss, well below the S&P 500’s 3.9% gain. This was partly due to its softer quarterly results and might have investors contemplating their next move.
